channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Ford Recalling 420,000 US Vehicles Over Seat-Belt Issue
item.description: Related: Stellantis Recalling 419K US Vehicles for Improper Side Air Bag Deployment As part of the remedy, the regulator said that dealers will inspect the seat belt retractors and replace them if necessary at no cost. Related: Honda Recalling 99,000 US Vehicles Over Airbag Issues The recall affects certain 2018-2022 Expedition and Lincoln Navigator vehicles, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said. Ford Motor will recall 419,967 vehicles in the U.S.

item.title: RIMS RISKWORLD Q&A: Swiss Re’s Adrian Hall on Risk, Resilience and the Future of Insurance
item.description: CEO Adrian Hall discusses the challenges and opportunities emerging across the property and casualty insurance market, including the growing demand for multinational program expertise, alternative risk transfer solutions and technical specialization. This interview took place at the May RIMS RISKWORLD conference in Philadelphia and has been edited slightly for clarity. Adrian Hall: Over the last period, we’ve seen the marketplace shift.

item.title: West Coast Chemical Emergencies Raise Questions About Massive Industrial Tank Safety
item.description: Yet this past week, there were two major hazardous chemical emergencies on the West Coast. After the Bhopal, India, disaster at a pesticide plant in 1984 that killed at least 3,800 people, the chemical industry took a number of steps to improve safety, including making sure chemical tanks are built right and inspected, informing workers about the risks and analyzing what could go wrong if the tank fails and who is at risk. And late last week about 50,000 people were evacuated in Southern California after a chemical tank overheated and threatened the area with a catastrophic explosion.

item.title: UN Researchers Project AI to Double Data Center Power, Water Consumption by 2030
item.description: Annual power consumption from data centers is projected to double to 945 TWh by 2030, around the same as the whole of Japan, with AI accounting for 40% of the total. Data centers are expected to consume twice as much power and water by 2030 as they expand to meet the surge in demand from artificial intelligence, U.N. The data center land footprint is also forecast to increase from 6,900 square km (2,664 square miles) last year to more than 14,500 square km by 2030, the report said.

item.title: FSB Regional Consultative Group for Europe meets in Vienna
item.description: The Financial Stability Board (FSB) Regional Consultative Group for Europe (RCG Europe) convened on 2-3 June 2026 in Vienna, hosted by Oesterreichische Nationalbank. Co-chaired by Antoine Martin, Vice Chairman of the Governing Board of Swiss National Bank, and Marja Nykänen, Member of the Board of the Bank of Finland, the meeting addressed the following topics: Members also participated in a roundtable discussion on private credit, focusing on its growth in Europe, liquidity risks, and vulnerabilities related to retail investor involvement in private credit funds. The meeting brought together senior officials from central banks, financial authorities, and regulatory bodies across the region to discuss key financial stability issues.

item.title: Anthropic Funding Pushed Startup Investment To Near-Record Levels In May As Exit Market Reopened
item.description: While Anthropic ’s $50 billion raise — the second-largest startup funding deal on record — pushed global startup investment to one of the highest monthly totals of all time, Cerebras Systems’ successful IPO previews a potential blockbuster infusion of liquidity back into the private markets that could fuel the next wave of startup investment. May set the stage for a new phase for the startup market. All told, global venture funding reached $92 billion in May, marking the second-largest monthly total on record, just behind February , Crunchbase data shows.

item.title: Insurance cover growing broadly in line with increasing natural catastrophe risk: Swiss Re
item.description: Swiss Re’s Institute explained, “We find insurance coverage is growing at a rate broadly in line with increasing natural catastrophe risk: our SRI Natural Catastrophe Insurance Resilience Index rose to about 27% in 2025, up from about 25% in 2015, a modest but significant improvement.” But, nearly three quarters of global exposure is still uninsured according to the research, highlighting the need for more risk capital, as well as efforts to expand insurance coverage around the world. While the global natural catastrophe insurance protection gap is estimated to have risen to US $424 billion in 2025, new research from Swiss Re’s Institute suggests that insurance coverage is growing at a rate broadly in line with the increasing catastrophe exposure. Swiss Re said that both insurance and adaptation efforts are critical to narrowing the global natural catastrophe protection gap and importantly the reinsurance company highlights that adaptation projects have a median projected benefit-cost ratio (BCR) of 1.86, a benefit of almost twice every US dollar invested.

item.title: El Niño shifts ILS probabilities in hurricane season, but supply-demand dictates spreads: Solidum
item.description: While a less active hurricane season typically implies downward pressure on nominal spreads, the firm highlights that a broader array of market forces, including capital supply-demand dynamics and total reinsurance capacity, will continue to influence market direction. In a new report released by the Swiss insurance-linked securities investment manager, which specifically examines how the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season coincides with the ILS market, Solidum outlines that while El Niño is a critical factor, it is only one of the many factors that are important for such a forecast. However, there are numerous other influencing factors, such as supply and demand, the total capital of insurance and reinsurance companies, and other variables,” Solidum Partners said.

item.title: RenRe’s partner and ILS capital up ~7% or $520m in last year, hit $8.46bn at March 31st
item.description: Again including RenRe’s co-investments, at the end of March 2026 DaVinci had $4.65 billion of capital and increased $250 million in the last year. RenaissanceRe Capital Partners, the specialist division of the reinsurer, lifted its third-party investor capital managed across joint-venture reinsurance and insurance-linked securities strategies by around 7% over the last year, to end March 2026 with $8.46 billion of assets under management. Now, with the total increasing to $8.46 billion at March 31st 2026, the figure stands almost 7% higher year-on-year, for an increase of approximately $520 million over the twelve months.

item.title: Adaptation projects essential for narrowing global nat cat protection gap: Swiss Re
item.description: A new report from Swiss Re has suggested that the rising value of natural catastrophe-exposed assets, and the resulting increase in the absolute protection gap, continues to pose resilience risks, even though insurance cover has broadly kept pace with rapidly growing exposures, highlighting the need for targeted adaptation projects that reduce expected losses, lower physical vulnerability, support insurability and improve the economics of risk transfer. According to the reinsurer’s new report, the global natural catastrophe protection gap reached $424 billion in 2025, up from $395 billion a year earlier. At the same time, Swiss Re observed that, if the long-term 5–7% annual real-term growth trend in insured losses persists, global insured losses could reach $186 billion by 2030.

item.title: DWF names Abbott Managing Partner of its Insurance Services division
item.description: DWF, a provider of integrated legal and business services, has announced the appointment of David Abbott as Managing Partner of its Insurance Services division, effective May 1, 2026. Paul Jenks, who has been appointed Chief Operating Officer, Insurance Services, will be supporting Abbott, who commented: “I’m delighted to take on the role of Managing Partner, Insurance Services. I look forward to working closely with Paul and the wider team as we take the division forward.” Abbott is a highly respected figure in the London insurance market, particularly specialising in reinsurance, political risk, political violence, trade credit, and energy classes.

item.title: Kin’s auto GWP surpasses $6m as home policies in force tops 250,000
item.description: Kin, the direct-to-consumer home and auto insurance company, has reported that auto gross written premiums (GWP) surpassed $6 million, while total home policies in force exceeded 250,000. Kin said the milestone of more than 250,000 home policies in force reflects continued growth across its 14-state footprint and an expanding customer base that is making auto cross-selling increasingly efficient. Reaching 250,000 home policies in force gives us a powerful foundation to scale that strategy efficiently, and we believe it positions Kin to compound growth and profitability as we expand auto into additional markets.”

item.title: USA Rare Earth to Invest $1.2B in South Carolina Facility to Boost Domestic Supply
item.description: USA Rare Earth said on Tuesday it will invest $1.2 billion to build a magnet manufacturing and rare earth metals facility in South Carolina, as it expands domestic production capacity. Rare earths are a group of elements used in electric vehicles, medical equipment, oil refining, wind turbines and defense. Shares of the company rose 5.9% to $31.1 in morning trade.

item.title: AI to Double Data Center Power, Water Use by 2030, UN Researchers Say
item.description: Data centers are expected to consume twice as much power and water by 2030 as they expand to meet the surge in demand from artificial intelligence, U.N. Unless governments heed the rising environmental costs of AI, the rapid rollout could also strain scarce land resources and create mountains of electronic waste, the United Nations University Institute for Water, Environment and Health warned in a report. (Reporting by David Stanway; Editing by Sherry Jacob-Phillips) Topics InsurTech Trends Data Driven Artificial Intelligence

item.title: People Moves: IMA Financial Names Price Head of Alabama Market
item.description: IMA Financial Group, a large North American insurance brokerage, named Rob Price as Alabama Market president, based in Birmingham, the firm announced. As Alabama market head he will oversee IMA’s Alabama offices, including growth and profitability, the company said. The Alabama market includes 86 employees, generates more than $24 million in annual revenue, and is part of IMA’s Southeast region.

item.title: Buddy launches bindable commercial insurance marketplace inside Stripe
item.description: Buddy has launched a new insurance distribution partnership with Stripe, enabling carriers and managing general agents (MGAs) to distribute bindable commercial insurance products directly to businesses operating on Stripe. Announced at the Insurtech Insights USA 2026 in New York, the launch introduces a new insurance collection within the Stripe App Marketplace, allowing businesses to apply for, quote, bind, and pay for commercial cover without leaving the Stripe platform. Through a Buddy-to-Stripe App protocol connection, any insurance product indexed on Buddy’s platform becomes immediately distributable to Stripe merchants without requiring additional technical integrations.

item.title: Decerto expands Claims AI platform in US as carriers shift from pilots to production automation
item.description: The post Decerto expands Claims AI platform in US as carriers shift from pilots to production automation appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Decerto has expanded the capabilities of its Claims AI platform for US property and casualty insurers, as carriers increasingly move beyond experimental AI deployments towards production-grade automation. The decision still belongs to the adjuster – that is a line we will not cross.” The expansion reflects a broader shift in the insurance market as carriers move from AI experimentation to operational deployment, particularly in claims functions where automation can materially improve cycle times, fraud detection, and customer experience while preserving human oversight.

item.title: Gallagher expands capital advisory team with Laura Repko hire
item.description: Gallagher UK appointed Laura Repko as Senior Director, Capital Solutions, within its global Private Equity and M&A practice as the company expands its transactional risk and capital advisory capabilities. Based in London, Repko will focus on insurance-backed capital solutions for lenders and borrowers across areas including credit enhancement, working capital financing, regulatory compliance, and capital event situations. She will work alongside Gallagher’s M&A Transactional Risk and Real Estate Advisory teams.

item.title: Lemonade brings Autonomous Car Insurance to Indiana
item.description: Lemonade announced that its Autonomous Car insurance is now available in Indiana, the third state since launching the coverage in January. Lemonade’s other car insurance coverage is available Arizona, California, Colorado, Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Oregon, Tennessee, Texas, and Washington. “Today, we’re bringing Lemonade Autonomous Car to Tesla drivers in Indiana.

item.title: Nippon Life partners with Blackstone on ~$10 billion private credit push
item.description: Nippon Life entered into a strategic partnership with Blackstone focused on private credit and real estate investments. Under the agreement, Nippon Life plans to allocate approximately ¥1.5 trillion in new capital to Blackstone-managed private and structured credit strategies over the next five years. As the world’s largest alternative asset manager with leading platforms in private credit and real estate, we will bring the full breadth of our capabilities to help advance Nippon Life group’s long-term objectives.” – Jon Gray, President & Chief Operating Officer, Blackstone.

item.title: Aegon moviliza a casi 300 empleados en una jornada solidaria con 19 iniciativas para colectivos vulnerables
item.description: Aegon reafirma que la solidaridad forma parte de su ADN corporativo. La aseguradora ha celebrado una nueva edición de su Día del Voluntariado, una iniciativa internacional que ha reunido en España a cerca de 300 empleados para participar en 19 proyectos solidarios desarrollados junto a 15 entidades sociales.

item.title: Juan Antonio Mata asume la Presidencia del Consejo Asesor de BMS Iberia
item.description: Según puede adelantar esta Redacción, BMS Iberia ha nombrado a Juan Antonio Mata presidente de su Consejo Asesor. El nombramiento coincide con el fin de su etapa ejecutiva como director general, cargo que ocupó tras la integración de la correduría Jurado Mata en 2020.

item.title: Grupo Peris activa una nueva área de gestión patrimonial y asesoramiento financiero
item.description: Grupo Peris ha lanzado una nueva área de gestión patrimonial, inversión y Vida-Ahorro con la que amplía su actividad hacia el asesoramiento financiero personalizado. La iniciativa está dirigida a clientes con capacidad de inversión a partir de 50.000 euros y busca ofrecer un modelo independiente de la banca privada tradicional.

item.title: Alphabet Planning to Raise $80 Billion for AI Goals, Berkshire to Invest $10B
item.description: Alphabet is looking to raise $80 billion in equity offerings, including via an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway, the Google parent said on Monday, in its aggressive push to fund a costly expansion of its AI infrastructure. Alphabet would sell $10 billion worth of shares to Berkshire in a private placement, comprising $5 billion in Class A common stock at $351.81 per share and $5 billion in Class C capital stock for $348.20 per share, both below Monday’s closing prices. In April, Alphabet raised its annual capital spending forecast by $5 billion to between $180 billion and $190 billion and said it was planning another significant increase in 2027.

item.title: AI Is Exposing Insurance’s Data Problem
item.description: According to the new Sollers Consulting CEO Voices Report 2026: AI and the Human Impact , insurance leaders increasingly view AI, automation, and data-driven decision-making as central to the future competitiveness of the industry. Artificial intelligence is rapidly becoming one of the insurance industry’s defining strategic priorities. For years, insurers have operated with fragmented systems, siloed business units, inconsistent data formats, disconnected workflows, and legacy infrastructures that evolved over decades.

item.title: AM Best upgrades Hong Kong reinsurer FuSure on strong balance sheet, Tencent backing
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Munich Re Specialty hires Bob Algie to lead Australia property, construction, engineering team
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Australia broker channel grows faster than non-life sector, posts 6.1% CAGR: NIBA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Football clubs warned about questionable sponsorship deals with unauthorised financial firms
item.description: Football clubs have been warned not to put their fans’ cash at risk by signing sponsorship deals with financial firms that aren't allowed to operate in the UK. Sponsorship deals with unauthorised financial services firms don't just harm fans. According to the FCA, a number of unauthorised firms, including crypto businesses and trading platforms, are using sponsorship to target unwitting football fans.

item.title: The Hanover secures 50% upsized $150m Commonwealth Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: has now successfully upsized and priced its fourth catastrophe bond sponsorship, securing $150 million of US-wide multi-peril reinsurance protection from the Commonwealth Re Ltd. We then reported on June 1st that The Hanover was aiming to upsize this Commonwealth Re Series 2026-1 offering by 50% to fill the full reinsurance layer it will occupy, providing $150 million of protection, while the price guidance was lowered. We first reported that The Hanover had returned to the cat bond market on May 14th .

item.title: California Earthquake Authority secures $425m reinsurance with Sutter Re 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: The California Earthquake Authority (CEA) came back to the cat bond market in early May with an initial target to secure $300 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance from the capital markets with this new Sutter Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond. With this new cat bond deal priced, the CEA has now secured a new $425 million source of multi-year reinsurance limit across the two tranches of cat bond notes from this Sutter Re 2026-1 offering, which will provide the insurer with a four year source of fully-collateralized California earthquake reinsurance protection on an indemnity trigger and annual aggregate basis. The California Earthquake Authority (CEA) has now successfully priced and secured its upsized target of $425 million of fully-collateralized earthquake reinsurance protection from its new Sutter Re Ltd.

item.title: Debut Quercian Re cat bond an ‘important moment’ for Oak Global, say Execs
item.description: Alex Winfield, Co-Founder, Chief Capital Officer and Head of Strategy for OAK Global, commented: “Entering the catastrophe bond market is an important moment in OAK Global’s development. With Oak Global successfully securing its debut catastrophe bond, the $150 million Arthur Re Ltd. – Quercian Re 2026-1 issuance, executives from the firm have highlighted how it marks an important moment in the development of the company and demonstrates its ability to access new forms of capital.

item.title: Simon Hunt appointed CEO of HDI Global UK & Ireland
item.description: Simon Hunt has been appointed as Chief Executive Officer of HDI Global UK & Ireland, effective 1 June 2026, after serving as interim CEO since February 2026 following the departure of Stephanie Ogden. As CEO, Hunt is set to lead the UK & Ireland business according to HDI Global’s Xcelerate29 strategy, focusing on targeted, profitable growth in property, casualty and specialty lines. Hunt, who previously was CFO for the UK & Ireland, started serving as interim CEO in February 2026.

item.title: Gallagher ups stake in leading MEA insurance brokerage to 49%
item.description: Global insurance brokerage, risk management and consulting firm Gallagher has increased its stake in ACE Gallagher Holding, a leading insurance brokerage in the Middle East and Africa that is majority owned by MIG Holding, from 30% to 49%. The global insurance brokerage added that ACE Gallagher has expertise in aviation, real estate, construction, oil & gas, financial lines and property risks and employs over 300 risk professionals. ACE Gallagher, which supports businesses with risk management and insurance solutions, is reportedly a “pioneer” in the MEA regional insurance industry.

item.title: OAK Global enters cat bond market with upsized $150m Quercian Re issuance
item.description: OAK Global, a global risk partner underwriting through two dedicated syndicates at Lloyd’s, has sponsored its debut catastrophe bond through an upsized $150 million Quercian Re 2026-1 issuance. The debut issuance attracted strong support from a diversified capital markets investor base, enabling OAK Global to secure a 100% upsized placement. Alex Winfield, Co-Founder, Chief Capital Officer and Head of Strategy for OAK Global, said, “Entering the catastrophe bond market is an important moment in OAK Global’s development.

item.title: US Looks to Unblock Hormuz With Quiet Version of Project Freedom
item.description: The effort marks a change in tactic from Trump’s previous effort, dubbed Project Freedom, that he rolled out in early May. A month after President Donald Trump announced — and then abandoned — a plan to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the US military is trying less public ways of protecting vessels in the vital waterway. In a social media post late last month, Centcom denied as “FALSE” reports that the US Navy “has restarted escorting or assisting commercial vessels during transits through the Strait of Hormuz.” After more evidence emerged in recent days that several vessels had gotten through, the command changed its messaging.

item.title: Okla. Workers’ Compensation Commission Warns of Scam Targeting Injured Workers
item.description: The Oklahoma Workers’ Compensation Commission is alerting residents of a scam targeting injured workers. Scammers have contacted injured workers by phone, text message, or social media by claiming to be a workers’ compensation attorney, according to the commission. Source: Oklahoma Workers’ Compensation Commission Topics Workers' Compensation Talent Oklahoma

item.title: Texas Lifts Bans on CDLs for Temporary Agriculture Workers
item.description: The change on Monday will allow those with H-2A visas, designed for temporary agriculture workers, to again hold valid CDLs in Texas. The Texas Department of Public Safety on Monday lifted a restriction on commercial drivers licenses and permits for immigrants with temporary agriculture work visas and removed the Spanish language option for CDL tests following changes in federal requirements. DPS indicated the shift was made in line with a new federal guideline from the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration published in mid-March, which permits H-2A workers to hold CDLs alongside H-2B and E-2 visa holders, which are temporary non-agricultural workers and treaty investors, respectively.

item.title: Cargo parametric insurer charts route to US$68bn protection gap with ‘new shiny object’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Hyland expands AI content platform across Asia-Pacific
item.description: Hyland expanded its AI-native Content Innovation Cloud across the Asia-Pacific region through its partnership with Amazon Web Services. The expansion brings Hyland’s platform to the AWS Asia Pacific (Sydney) Region, allowing organizations in regulated industries including insurance, healthcare, financial services, education, and government to deploy AI-driven content management capabilities while meeting local data residency requirements. The platform is designed to support “agentic enterprise” operations by combining enterprise content management, AI orchestration, and automation tools.

item.title: Boston Mutual Life appoints EVP of distribution
item.description: Boston Mutual Life Insurance appointed Jack Douglas as Executive Vice President of Distribution, effective June 8, 2026. Boston Mutual Life said its distribution team focuses on workplace insurance products including life, accident, disability, and critical illness coverage, serving customers such as union members, public sector employees, first responders, teachers, and small businesses through broker partnerships. He joins Boston Mutual Life from Assurity, where he most recently served as Executive Vice President of Worksite Sales.

item.title: Integrity da el salto: así será su estrategia para conquistar el crédito empresarial en Colombia
item.description: El neobanco Integrity, que llegó a Colombia hace dos años , obtuvo la autorización de la Superintendencia Financiera para operar como compañía de financiamiento , una licencia que activará en el segundo semestre de este año. Con ello, las pequeñas y medianas empresas podrán acceder a los servicios de factoring , confirming , working capital y crédito bilateral o corporativo. “Nos enfocaremos en compañías pequeñas y medianas en crecimiento, especialmente aquellas que están en una etapa en la que requieren soluciones más sofisticadas de liquidez, capital de trabajo y acompañamiento financiero, pero que muchas veces no encuentran una oferta suficientemente flexible o especializada”, dijo David Rey, CEO de Integrity Corporación Financiera.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing the Global Gas Trade into the Shadows
item.description: “You look at all those chokepoints, and all of a sudden the foundation of seaborne international global trade just goes out the window. The Al Rayyan, loaded with Qatari LNG, tailed another gas carrier coming from the emirate, the Fuwairit, which was preparing to cross the waterway under a deal between Pakistan and Iran. When US and Israeli strikes on Iran began in late February, Qatar was among the first producers to find itself caught in the economic crossfire.

item.title: 100 Days of Summer Teen Driving Safety Campaign Begins
item.description: This summer, AAA is urging drivers to commit to “100 Days of Safe Driving” between Memorial Day and Labor Day. “The ‘100 Days of Safe Driving’ campaign is about encouraging drivers to make simple choices behind the wheel that can help save lives,” said Gene Boehm, president and CEO of AAA. According to its review of national crash data, more than 30% of fatal crashes involving teen drivers and nearly one-third of all impaired-driving fatalities occur between Memorial Day and Labor Day.

item.title: USQRisk appoints Chris Giuffre as President following leadership changes
item.description: USQRisk, a managing general underwriter within Ryan Alternative Risk, a division of Ryan Specialty, has announced the promotion of Chris Giuffre to President of USQRisk with immediate effect. Chris’ experience developing unique programs and convergence products align with our growth strategy to affirm our position as a leading alternative risk transfer underwriter.” Giuffre added: “I’m honoured to step into this expanded role as President of USQRisk. The company noted that following this change, Michael Zipper will step away from the President role but will continue in his position as Co-CEO of USQRisk alongside Parag Abvishi, Co-CEO and Chief Underwriting Officer.

item.title: Business Moves: WTW Buys Redefind to Boost Access to Crypto and Digital Asset Insurance Offering; ASR Agrees to Strategic Investment From PE Firm Vitruvian Partners
item.description: WTW Buys Redefind to Boost Access to Crypto, Digital Asset Insurance Insurance broker WTW announced it has acquisition of Redefind , an end-to-end web-based platform, designed to facilitate access to insurance products for crypto and digital assets. WTW said this investment reflects its long-term strategy to expand into next-generation protection solutions for clients exposed to digital finance, crypto ecosystems and tokenized asset environments. The proposition launches as a non-custodial, cost-of-recovery insurance solution, intended to support digital asset owners in the event of theft or loss.

item.title: Sentry to Sponsor PGA Tournament at Torrey Pines
item.description: Sentry Insurance will take over as the sponsor of the PGA Tour event at Torrey Pines Golf Course in San Diego, Calif. The PGA, meanwhile, needed a new sponsor for its Torrey Pines tournament after Farmers Insurance chose not to renew its contract as lead sponsor. “For 75 years the PGA TOUR has hosted elite competition in San Diego, including the last 60 at revered Torrey Pines, and we are proud to build upon that legacy and longtime philanthropic impact with our partners at Sentry Insurance,” said PGA TOUR CEO Brian Rolapp.

item.title: Northeast States Sue Trump Administration Over Offshore Wind Farm Cancellation
item.description: states led by New York sued the Trump administration on Tuesday for canceling a major offshore wind lease off the coast of New York in exchange for a pledge by its owner to invest instead in fossil fuel projects. Department of the Interior to cancel a lease by a subsidiary of France’s TotalEnergies, “reimburse” $795 million to the company, and extract a pledge from the company not to develop new offshore wind projects in the United States. offshore wind projects, which President Donald Trump has said he finds ugly and expensive.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Complicates Winery Insurance
item.description: Smoke taint claims turn on whether wineries can document when grapes were exposed during increasingly overlapping wildfire and harvest seasons. A vineyard or winery may avoid direct fire damage and still face critical economic loss if smoke exposure affects grape quality during harvest operations or while harvested fruit is awaiting processing. As a result, smoke exposure is becoming a growing risk issue not only for viticulture and winemaking teams but also for insurance brokers, adjusters, and coverage counsel evaluating when a claimed loss occurred and how the condition of the product can be established.

item.title: Report: U.S. States' Fiscal Outlook Is Stable
item.description: But Conning's latest State of the States report finds greater divergence as post-pandemic momentum fades and structural regional differences deepen. Conning maintains a stable outlook for U.S. states in 2026, reflecting continued balance sheet strength and generally prudent fiscal management across, even as operating conditions become more challenging.
